'''Herb Trimpe''' (d. [[April 13]], [[2015]]<ref name="Death" />) was a [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] artist who worked on a few issues of the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']] series. Trimpe joined [[Marvel Comics]] in the late [[1960s]] and worked on a number of titles, most notably [[Wikipedia:The Incredible Hulk|''The Incredible Hulk'']] where he co-created the character [[Wikipedia:Wolverine (character)|Wolverine]]. Trimpe was let go by Marvel as the company was having financial troubles in [[1996]]. He was also an ordained deacon and wrote a book called ''The Power of Angels'' about his experiences as a chaplain during the months following the terrorist attacks in New York on [[September 11]], [[2001]].
